message:
feat(runtime): add hooks subsystem, scheduler extensions, protothread tests, and conformance suite

Concurrent agent work adding substantial runtime functionality:
- hooks.c: Full hook registration and dispatch subsystem (~1262 lines)
  for lifecycle, scheduler, and I/O event interception
- lifecycle.c: Extended obligation arena with hook integration points
- runtime.h: Public API for hook registration and protothread scheduling
- scheduler.c: Hook-aware scheduling extensions
- New conformance test suite and protothread unit tests
- CI tools for layout budget reporting and delta computation
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-66l.9","title":"Enforce per-bead evidence linkage contract for unit/e2e/logging completeness","description":"Background:
The hard-part verification rules require every semantic-sensitive implementation bead to carry explicit proof linkage, not only CI-level gating.

Subtasks:
- Define required evidence fields per implementation bead: linked unit tests, linked e2e scripts, expected artifact manifests, and replay/parity references.
- Require linkage against the unit/invariant completeness matrix (bd-1md.12) and canonical e2e suite contract (bd-1md.10) so evidence remains coverage-complete, not ad hoc.
- Add validation tooling that checks bead metadata and notes for required evidence references before status transitions to done.
- Integrate checks with anti-butchering proof-block gate and traceability index updates.
- Provide reviewer/operator guidance for interpreting evidence packs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive beads cannot be marked complete without linked unit/e2e/logging evidence.
- Evidence packs reference explicit unit/invariant obligations and at least one canonical e2e lane per covered semantic surface.
- Validation failures are explicit and actionable.
- Evidence links remain machine-checkable for audit and release workflows.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:18.357456678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.739040975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","quality-gates","testing","traceability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:07:48.904266460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.738995740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:07:48.996785099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:22.759321886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:07:49.091346475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:31:11.774586969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:08:33.223238282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:22.657163881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:14:43.309178413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:14:43.410120636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":  {"id":"bd-hwb","title":"EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99","description":"Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:19:04.939650111Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","safety","semantics"]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.1","title":"Implement core API types: status, errors, outcomes, budgets, cancellation, and IDs","description":"Background:
These primitives are shared across all runtime behavior and therefore must be stable, deterministic, and audit-friendly.

Subtasks:
- Define status/error taxonomy with stable code families.
- Define outcome types and ordering semantics.
- Define budget and cancellation core types with explicit failure semantics.
- Define ID formats and generation strategy for handle safety.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Types are fully documented and covered by unit tests.
- Error/status semantics are deterministic and profile-agnostic.
- Public API naming and visibility are consistent.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.812710740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652897529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652875979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","core","phase-3","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.398405303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:45.727570861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.812710740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.477855807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:50.978642851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hwb.10","title":"Implement protothread-style task poll contract and region-arena captured-state safety","description":"Background:
Plan sections 6.8.A and 7.4 require a safe C replacement for async/await that preserves lifecycle semantics without stack-lifetime footguns.

Subtasks:
- Implement coroutine-style task macros (`ASX_CO_BEGIN`, `ASX_CO_YIELD`, `ASX_CO_END`) with explicit resume state.
- Enforce region-arena ownership of captured task state; no malloc-owned suspended state in kernel profiles.
- Add explicit yield-safety lint gate that flags non-const locals/raw internal pointers persisting across yield unless waived.
- Define spawn contract for state sizing/finalization so suspended tasks cannot leak on region close.
- Add tests for suspend/resume ordering, cancellation checkpoints, and region-close cleanup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Poll contract is deterministic and fixture-tested.
- No leaked task state on cancellation or region closure.
- Yield-safety violations are caught automatically by CI lint/static checks.
- Macro workflow is documented with explicit do/dont examples.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:06.942908349Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coroutines","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.358282142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.436329661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-hwb.10","title":"Implement protothread-style task poll contract and region-arena captured-state safety","description":"Background:
Plan sections 6.8.A and 7.4 require a safe C replacement for async/await that preserves lifecycle semantics without stack-lifetime footguns.

Subtasks:
- Implement coroutine-style task macros (`ASX_CO_BEGIN`, `ASX_CO_YIELD`, `ASX_CO_END`) with explicit resume state.
- Enforce region-arena ownership of captured task state; no malloc-owned suspended state in kernel profiles.
- Add explicit yield-safety lint gate that flags non-const locals/raw internal pointers persisting across yield unless waived.
- Define spawn contract for state sizing/finalization so suspended tasks cannot leak on region close.
- Add tests for suspend/resume ordering, cancellation checkpoints, and region-close cleanup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Poll contract is deterministic and fixture-tested.
- No leaked task state on cancellation or region closure.
- Yield-safety violations are caught automatically by CI lint/static checks.
- Macro workflow is documented with explicit do/dont examples.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:32:47.371948620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coroutines","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.358282142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.436329661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.11","title":"Implement thread-affinity guards and explicit cross-thread transfer certificates","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.C requires a concrete replacement for Rust Send and Sync protections.

Subtasks:
- Add affinity domain tags to mutable runtime-owned objects.
- Add debug/hardened boundary checks that trap wrong-thread mutable access deterministically.
- Implement explicit transfer API with atomic ownership handoff metadata.
- Add misuse tests for stale-domain access, double-transfer, and missing-transfer paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cross-thread misuse fails deterministically with stable error diagnostics.
- Legitimate transfer path is explicit, auditable, and replay-visible.
- Safety checks are profile-gated with documented production overhead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:18.842708002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.520412450Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","core","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:18.842708002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.520357637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.679601504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.12","title":"Implement ghost borrow ledger and ghost determinism monitor","description":"Background:
Plan sections 6.10 and 6.8.E/H call for debug-only ghost systems that recover lost Rust confidence.

Subtasks:
- Implement ghost borrow ledger with shared and exclusive epoch tracking per handle.
- Reject exclusive writes while shared borrow epochs are active.
- Implement ghost determinism monitor that validates scheduler key ordering and per-run digest stability.
- Wire violation events into structured diagnostics and replay artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Known borrow/protocol violations are detected with deterministic repro logs.
- Determinism monitor catches ordering drift in dedicated tests.
- Debug/hardened profiles enable monitors; release profile behavior is explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:18.922496934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.600523315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ghost","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.12","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:18.922496934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.12","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.512550879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.12","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.600468773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.12","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.843505554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.13","title":"Implement runtime hook contract for allocator, reactor, clock, entropy, and log sinks","description":"Background:
Plan sections 7.6 and 7.10 require explicit runtime hooks plus deterministic-mode boundary enforcement.

Subtasks:
- Define hook contract for allocator, reactor, clock, entropy, and logging callbacks.
- Enforce deterministic-mode rules: no ambient entropy and logical-time/recorded-event boundary behavior.
- Add ghost-reactor replay mode for deterministic readiness replays.
- Implement optional steady-state allocator seal (`asx_runtime_seal_allocator`) for hardened/no-allocation profiles.
- Add validation tests for missing hooks, invalid hook behavior, deterministic constraints, and allocator-seal traps.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Hook contract is fully documented and test-covered.
- Deterministic mode rejects forbidden entropy/reactor/time paths with stable errors.
- Replay mode remains semantically equivalent to live mode for shared fixtures.
- Allocator-seal behavior is profile-gated and regression-tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.005967975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:58:30.621284520Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:58:30.621262979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","determinism","platform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.13","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:40.006722717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.13","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.005967975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.13","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:40.085644204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hwb.2","title":"Implement and verify outcome severity lattice plus budget algebra/exhaustion semantics","description":"Background:
Outcome joins and budget rules are central semantic contracts that must exactly match extracted behavior.

Subtasks:
- Implement outcome join and severity comparison operations.
- Implement budget composition, reduction, and exhaustion behavior.
- Add edge-case tests for empty/max/boundary and conflicting outcomes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit tests demonstrate deterministic and associative behavior where required.
- Exhaustion semantics are explicit and failure-atomic.
- Matches canonical fixture expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.895345522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:11:34.192984085Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:11:34.192956734Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","invariants","math","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.895345522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.560288881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.3","title":"Implement transition authority tables for region/task/obligation/cancellation state machines","description":"Background:
State-machine authority tables are the primary substitute for compile-time typestate guarantees.

Subtasks:
- Encode legal transitions and explicit forbidden transitions.
- Enforce preconditions and postconditions on each transition API.
- Generate transition legality tests from invariant schema where possible.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every lifecycle domain has explicit transition map.
- Illegal transitions return stable deterministic errors.
- Transition tests include positive and negative paths.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.976947939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:12:15.003704202Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:12:15.003678113Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.726175963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.976947939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.643270002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.300454014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.4","title":"Implement cleanup-stack and ownership/handle safety mechanisms","description":"Background:
C lacks RAII and borrow checking. We need explicit mechanisms to preserve lifecycle and aliasing discipline.

Subtasks:
- Implement cleanup-stack primitives for deterministic unwind and rollback.
- Implement generation-based handle validation and stale-handle detection.
- Implement optional debug quarantine mode for reuse hardening.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cleanup behavior is deterministic and failure-atomic.
- Handle misuse paths are test-covered with explicit errors.
- Ownership assumptions are documented in API contract notes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.058393232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:55.844150859Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:55.844129940Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","core","ownership","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.058393232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.148594467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.704593093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.807706856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hwb.5","title":"Implement ghost safety monitors for protocol and linearity invariants","description":"Background:
Ghost monitors provide lightweight runtime checks to recover part of Rust-level protocol confidence in debug/hardened modes.

Subtasks:
- Implement protocol monitor for lifecycle sequencing violations.
- Implement linearity monitor for reserve/commit/abort misuse.
- Integrate monitor outputs into test diagnostics and traces.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Monitor violations are deterministic and actionable.
- Overhead behavior is documented per safety profile.
- Tests demonstrate detection of known violation patterns.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:40.141869469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ghost","invariants","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.240868242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.611705988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.888389910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.660062513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hwb.6","title":"Implement strict resource-contract engine and deterministic exhaustion behavior","description":"Background:
Resource exhaustion is a major C failure mode. The plan requires deterministic, non-corrupting behavior at all boundaries.

Subtasks:
- Implement configurable ceilings (memory, queue depth, timer nodes, trace events).
- Implement admission/backpressure/reject decision points with stable error codes.
- Add failure-atomic rollback semantics at boundary transitions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No partial state corruption on exhaustion paths.
- Boundary tests cover arena alloc, queue growth, timer ops, and obligation ops.
- Behavior is consistent across profiles and reproducible in replay.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.229496469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.789338346Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","oom","phase-3","resource"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:41.793538869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:11.050816887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.229496469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.968580161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.747564647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.244601157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.336951536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Implement compiler-enforced must-use behavior for transition/acquisition APIs.
- Implement zero-allocation contextual error ledger (`file/line/op`) for deterministic failure propagation breadcrumbs.

- Implement deterministic time/entropy/clock-anomaly fault injection hooks and fixtures.

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+Usage: compute_layout_budget_delta.sh
+  --run-id <id>
+  --index-file <path>
+  --output <path>
+
+Index file format (tab-separated):
+  <target_id>\t<kv_report_path>
+
+Produces a deterministic JSON delta report and exits with:
+  0  success, no invariant regressions
+  3  success, invariant regressions flagged
+  1  error while computing report
+EOF
+}
+
+json_escape() {
+  printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g'
+}
+
+is_invariant_metric() {
+  case "$1" in
+    size.asx_region_id|\
+    size.asx_task_id|\
+    size.asx_obligation_id|\
+    size.asx_timer_id|\
+    size.asx_channel_id|\
+    size.asx_time|\
+    size.asx_status|\
+    size.asx_outcome_severity|\
+    size.asx_cancel_kind|\
+    size.asx_cancel_phase|\
+    size.asx_budget|\
+    budget.cleanup_stack_capacity|\
+    budget.error_ledger_depth|\
+    budget.error_ledger_task_slots)
+      return 0
+      ;;
+    *)
+      return 1
+      ;;
+  esac
+}
+
+load_metrics() {
+  local kv_path="$1"
+  local map_name="$2"
+  declare -n map_ref="$map_name"
+
+  while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
+    case "$key" in
+      size.*|align.*|budget.*)
+        if [[ "$value" =~ ^-?[0-9]+$ ]]; then
+          map_ref["$key"]="$value"
+        fi
+        ;;
+      *)
+        ;;
+    esac
+  done <"$kv_path"
+}
+
+run_id=""
+index_file=""
+output_path=""
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --run-id)
+      run_id="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --index-file)
+      index_file="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --output)
+      output_path="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    -h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+if [[ -z "$run_id" || -z "$index_file" || -z "$output_path" ]]; then
+  echo "Missing required arguments" >&2
+  usage >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+if [[ ! -f "$index_file" ]]; then
+  echo "Index file not found: $index_file" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/asx-layout-delta.XXXXXX")"
+cleanup() {
+  rm -rf "$tmp_dir"
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+entries_file="$tmp_dir/entries.tsv"
+differences_file="$tmp_dir/differences.tsv"
+regressions_file="$tmp_dir/regressions.tsv"
+invariants_file="$tmp_dir/invariants.txt"
+
+grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' "$index_file" >"$entries_file" || true
+entry_count="$(wc -l <"$entries_file" | tr -d ' ')"
+
+cat >"$invariants_file" <<'EOF'
+size.asx_region_id
+size.asx_task_id
+size.asx_obligation_id
+size.asx_timer_id
+size.asx_channel_id
+size.asx_time
+size.asx_status
+size.asx_outcome_severity
+size.asx_cancel_kind
+size.asx_cancel_phase
+size.asx_budget
+budget.cleanup_stack_capacity
+budget.error_ledger_depth
+budget.error_ledger_task_slots
+EOF
+
+if [[ "$entry_count" -lt 2 ]]; then
+  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$output_path")"
+  {
+    printf '{\n'
+    printf '  "schema": "asx.layout_budget_delta.v1",\n'
+    printf '  "run_id": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$run_id")"
+    printf '  "status": "insufficient_data",\n'
+    printf '  "targets_compared": %s,\n' "$entry_count"
+    printf '  "regression_count": 0,\n'
+    printf '  "difference_count": 0,\n'
+    printf '  "regressions": [],\n'
+    printf '  "differences": []\n'
+    printf '}\n'
+  } >"$output_path"
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+IFS=$'\t' read -r baseline_target baseline_kv <"$entries_file"
+if [[ -z "$baseline_target" || -z "$baseline_kv" || ! -f "$baseline_kv" ]]; then
+  echo "Invalid baseline entry in $index_file" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+declare -A baseline_metrics
+load_metrics "$baseline_kv" baseline_metrics
+
+: >"$differences_file"
+: >"$regressions_file"
+
+tail -n +2 "$entries_file" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r target kv_path; do
+  [[ -z "$target" || -z "$kv_path" ]] && continue
+  if [[ ! -f "$kv_path" ]]; then
+    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$target" "__missing_report__" "0" "0" "0" >>"$regressions_file"
+    continue
+  fi
+
+  declare -A target_metrics=()
+  load_metrics "$kv_path" target_metrics
+
+  for metric in "${!baseline_metrics[@]}"; do
+    baseline_value="${baseline_metrics[$metric]}"
+    target_value="${target_metrics[$metric]:-}"
+    if [[ -z "$target_value" ]]; then
+      printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$target" "$metric" "$baseline_value" "0" "0" >>"$regressions_file"
+      continue
+    fi
+
+    delta=$((target_value - baseline_value))
+    if [[ "$delta" -ne 0 ]]; then
+      printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$target" "$metric" "$baseline_value" "$target_value" "$delta" >>"$differences_file"
+      if is_invariant_metric "$metric"; then
+        printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$target" "$metric" "$baseline_value" "$target_value" "$delta" >>"$regressions_file"
+      fi
+    fi
+  done
+done
+
+difference_count="$(wc -l <"$differences_file" | tr -d ' ')"
+regression_count="$(wc -l <"$regressions_file" | tr -d ' ')"
+
+status="pass"
+if [[ "$regression_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
+  status="regression"
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$(dirname "$output_path")"
+{
+  printf '{\n'
+  printf '  "schema": "asx.layout_budget_delta.v1",\n'
+  printf '  "run_id": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$run_id")"
+  printf '  "baseline_target": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$baseline_target")"
+  printf '  "status": "%s",\n' "$status"
+  printf '  "targets_compared": %s,\n' "$entry_count"
+  printf '  "difference_count": %s,\n' "$difference_count"
+  printf '  "regression_count": %s,\n' "$regression_count"
+
+  printf '  "invariant_metrics": [\n'
+  inv_first=1
+  while IFS= read -r invariant; do
+    [[ -z "$invariant" ]] && continue
+    if [[ "$inv_first" -eq 0 ]]; then
+      printf ',\n'
+    fi
+    printf '    "%s"' "$(json_escape "$invariant")"
+    inv_first=0
+  done <"$invariants_file"
+  printf '\n  ],\n'
+
+  printf '  "regressions": [\n'
+  reg_first=1
+  while IFS=$'\t' read -r target metric baseline_value target_value delta; do
+    [[ -z "$target" ]] && continue
+    if [[ "$reg_first" -eq 0 ]]; then
+      printf ',\n'
+    fi
+    printf '    {"target":"%s","metric":"%s","baseline":%s,"value":%s,"delta":%s}' \
+      "$(json_escape "$target")" \
+      "$(json_escape "$metric")" \
+      "$baseline_value" \
+      "$target_value" \
+      "$delta"
+    reg_first=0
+  done <"$regressions_file"
+  printf '\n  ],\n'
+
+  printf '  "differences": [\n'
+  diff_first=1
+  while IFS=$'\t' read -r target metric baseline_value target_value delta; do
+    [[ -z "$target" ]] && continue
+    if [[ "$diff_first" -eq 0 ]]; then
+      printf ',\n'
+    fi
+    printf '    {"target":"%s","metric":"%s","baseline":%s,"value":%s,"delta":%s}' \
+      "$(json_escape "$target")" \
+      "$(json_escape "$metric")" \
+      "$baseline_value" \
+      "$target_value" \
+      "$delta"
+    diff_first=0
+  done <"$differences_file"
+  printf '\n  ]\n'
+  printf '}\n'
+} >"$output_path"
+
+if [[ "$regression_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
+  exit 3
+fi
+exit 0

@@ -0,0 +1,285 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'EOF'
+Usage: generate_layout_budget_report.sh
+  --compiler <compiler>
+  --target-id <id>
+  --profile <CORE|POSIX|WIN32|FREESTANDING|EMBEDDED_ROUTER|HFT|AUTOMOTIVE>
+  --out-dir <dir>
+  [--bits <32|64>]
+  [--resource-class <R1|R2|R3|none>]
+  [--run-id <id>]
+  [--extra-cflags "<flags>"]
+
+Generates deterministic compile-time memory-layout and budget reports:
+  - <out-dir>/<run-id>-<target-id>-layout-budget.kv
+  - <out-dir>/<run-id>-<target-id>-layout-budget.json
+
+Prints the JSON report path on stdout on success.
+EOF
+}
+
+json_escape() {
+  printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g'
+}
+
+to_decimal() {
+  local raw="$1"
+  if [[ "$raw" =~ ^-?[0-9]+$ || "$raw" =~ ^-?0[xX][0-9a-fA-F]+$ ]]; then
+    printf '%d' "$((raw))"
+    return 0
+  fi
+  return 1
+}
+
+compiler=""
+target_id=""
+profile="CORE"
+out_dir=""
+bits=""
+resource_class="none"
+run_id="layout-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+extra_cflags=""
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --compiler)
+      compiler="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --target-id)
+      target_id="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --profile)
+      profile="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --out-dir)
+      out_dir="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --bits)
+      bits="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --resource-class)
+      resource_class="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --run-id)
+      run_id="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --extra-cflags)
+      extra_cflags="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    -h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+if [[ -z "$compiler" || -z "$target_id" || -z "$out_dir" ]]; then
+  echo "Missing required arguments" >&2
+  usage >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+repo_root="$(cd "$script_dir/../.." && pwd)"
+
+mkdir -p "$out_dir"
+
+safe_target="${target_id//\//_}"
+stem="${run_id}-${safe_target}-layout-budget"
+asm_path="$out_dir/${stem}.s"
+log_path="$out_dir/${stem}.log"
+kv_path="$out_dir/${stem}.kv"
+json_path="$out_dir/${stem}.json"
+
+tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/asx-layout-probe.XXXXXX")"
+cleanup() {
+  rm -rf "$tmp_dir"
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+probe_src="$tmp_dir/probe.c"
+raw_values="$tmp_dir/raw-values.txt"
+metrics_sorted="$tmp_dir/metrics-sorted.txt"
+metrics_lines="$tmp_dir/metrics-lines.txt"
+
+cat >"$probe_src" <<'EOF'
+#include <stddef.h>
+#include <asx/asx.h>
+#include <asx/core/cleanup.h>
+
+#define ASX_ALIGNOF(T) ((unsigned long)offsetof(struct { char c; T v; }, v))
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_region_id = sizeof(asx_region_id);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_task_id = sizeof(asx_task_id);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_obligation_id = sizeof(asx_obligation_id);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_timer_id = sizeof(asx_timer_id);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_channel_id = sizeof(asx_channel_id);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_time = sizeof(asx_time);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_status = sizeof(asx_status);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_outcome_severity = sizeof(asx_outcome_severity);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_cancel_kind = sizeof(asx_cancel_kind);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_cancel_phase = sizeof(asx_cancel_phase);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_void_ptr = sizeof(void *);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_size_t = sizeof(size_t);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_budget = sizeof(asx_budget);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_budget = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_budget);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_outcome = sizeof(asx_outcome);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_outcome = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_outcome);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_cancel_reason = sizeof(asx_cancel_reason);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_cancel_reason = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_cancel_reason);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_runtime_config = sizeof(asx_runtime_config);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_runtime_config = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_runtime_config);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_runtime_hooks = sizeof(asx_runtime_hooks);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_runtime_hooks = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_runtime_hooks);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_error_ledger_entry = sizeof(asx_error_ledger_entry);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_error_ledger_entry = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_error_ledger_entry);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_size_asx_cleanup_stack = sizeof(asx_cleanup_stack);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_align_asx_cleanup_stack = ASX_ALIGNOF(asx_cleanup_stack);
+
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_cleanup_stack_capacity = ASX_CLEANUP_STACK_CAPACITY;
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_error_ledger_depth = ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_DEPTH;
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_error_ledger_task_slots = ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_TASK_SLOTS;
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_error_ledger_capacity_entries =
+    (unsigned long)ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_DEPTH * (unsigned long)ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_TASK_SLOTS;
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_error_ledger_entry_bytes = sizeof(asx_error_ledger_entry);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_error_ledger_capacity_bytes =
+    (unsigned long)ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_DEPTH * (unsigned long)ASX_ERROR_LEDGER_TASK_SLOTS *
+    (unsigned long)sizeof(asx_error_ledger_entry);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_cleanup_stack_bytes = sizeof(asx_cleanup_stack);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_runtime_config_bytes = sizeof(asx_runtime_config);
+const unsigned long asx_probe_budget_runtime_hooks_bytes = sizeof(asx_runtime_hooks);
+EOF
+
+declare -a compile_cmd
+compile_cmd=("$compiler" "-std=c99" "-I$repo_root/include" "-DASX_PROFILE_${profile}")
+if [[ -n "$bits" ]]; then
+  compile_cmd+=("-m${bits}")
+fi
+if [[ -n "$extra_cflags" ]]; then
+  # shellcheck disable=SC2206
+  extra_tokens=($extra_cflags)
+  compile_cmd+=("${extra_tokens[@]}")
+fi
+compile_cmd+=("-S" "-o" "$asm_path" "$probe_src")
+
+if ! "${compile_cmd[@]}" >"$log_path" 2>&1; then
+  echo "layout probe compile failed for target '$target_id' (see $log_path)" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+awk '
+  {
+    if (match($0, /^[[:space:]]*(asx_probe_[A-Za-z0-9_]+):[[:space:]]*$/, m)) {
+      symbol = m[1];
+      next;
+    }
+    if (symbol != "" &&
+        match($0, /^[[:space:]]*\.(quad|long|word|byte|2byte|4byte|8byte|xword|dword)[[:space:]]+([^[:space:],#]+)/, m)) {
+      print symbol "=" m[2];
+      symbol = "";
+    }
+  }
+' "$asm_path" >"$raw_values"
+
+if [[ ! -s "$raw_values" ]]; then
+  echo "layout probe parse failed for target '$target_id' (no probe constants found in $asm_path)" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+{
+  echo "schema=asx.layout_budget.v1"
+  echo "run_id=$run_id"
+  echo "target_id=$target_id"
+  echo "compiler=$compiler"
+  echo "profile=ASX_PROFILE_${profile}"
+  if [[ -n "$bits" ]]; then
+    echo "bits=$bits"
+  else
+    echo "bits=unknown"
+  fi
+  echo "resource_class=$resource_class"
+  echo "asm_path=$asm_path"
+} >"$kv_path"
+
+while IFS='=' read -r symbol raw_value; do
+  metric=""
+  case "$symbol" in
+    asx_probe_size_*)
+      metric="size.${symbol#asx_probe_size_}"
+      ;;
+    asx_probe_align_*)
+      metric="align.${symbol#asx_probe_align_}"
+      ;;
+    asx_probe_budget_*)
+      metric="budget.${symbol#asx_probe_budget_}"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      continue
+      ;;
+  esac
+
+  if ! value="$(to_decimal "$raw_value")"; then
+    echo "non-numeric probe value '$raw_value' for '$symbol'" >&2
+    exit 1
+  fi
+  printf '%s=%s\n' "$metric" "$value" >>"$metrics_lines"
+done <"$raw_values"
+
+sort "$metrics_lines" >"$metrics_sorted"
+cat "$metrics_sorted" >>"$kv_path"
+
+{
+  printf '{\n'
+  printf '  "schema": "asx.layout_budget.v1",\n'
+  printf '  "run_id": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$run_id")"
+  printf '  "target_id": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$target_id")"
+  printf '  "compiler": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$compiler")"
+  printf '  "profile": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "ASX_PROFILE_${profile}")"
+  if [[ -n "$bits" ]]; then
+    printf '  "bits": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$bits")"
+  else
+    printf '  "bits": "unknown",\n'
+  fi
+  printf '  "resource_class": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$resource_class")"
+  printf '  "asm_path": "%s",\n' "$(json_escape "$asm_path")"
+  printf '  "metrics": [\n'
+
+  first=1
+  while IFS='=' read -r metric value; do
+    [[ -z "$metric" ]] && continue
+    if [[ "$first" -eq 0 ]]; then
+      printf ',\n'
+    fi
+    printf '    {"name":"%s","value":%s}' "$(json_escape "$metric")" "$value"
+    first=0
+  done <"$metrics_sorted"
+
+  printf '\n  ]\n'
+  printf '}\n'
+} >"$json_path"
+
+printf '%s\n' "$json_path"
